assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md contains '## Classification: code change request'. report.md confirms '30001 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs:60 | code change request | Sub-task created'. subtask-30001.md exists with the sub-task content.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains '## Classification: suggestion'. The reasoning section explains: 'The reviewer proposes adding a partial index as a performance optimization. While the language uses "should," the feedback is a performance suggestion rather than a correctness requirement -- the code functions correctly without the index.' It also notes 'The reviewer provides reasoning ("queries filtering by `deleted_at IS NULL` will be frequent")' which covers the suggestive language analysis.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md contains '## Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task is created.' report.md confirms '30003 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs:18 | nit | No sub-task'. No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md contains '## Classification: question' and 'No sub-task is created from this classification'. report.md confirms '30004 | modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs:1 | question | No sub-task'. No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 11), '## Implementation Notes' (line 14), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 20). Only one sub-task was created (subtask-30001.md), so all sub-tasks satisfy this assertion.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (lines 35-36).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 28) with the original comment text: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction. If the sbom_advisory update fails after sbom_package succeeds, you'll have inconsistent state. Wrap the three operations in `self.db.transaction(|txn| { ... })` and use `txn` instead of `self.db` for each exec call.' (lines 32-33)

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), a sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and the report (report.md). No source code files (.rs, .toml, .sql, etc.) appear in the outputs. All output files are markdown reports/classifications.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md references all four comments in the Review Comment Classifications table and the Review Feedback row, confirming all were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no merge actions. The overall result is 'FAIL' with issues requiring attention. The report ends with the AI-generated watermark. There is no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge command/action anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are new (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs). No modified or deleted test files. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md reasoning explicitly evaluates convention upgrade eligibility: 'No CONVENTIONS.md exists in the target repository, and there is no demonstrated codebase pattern of consistent index creation in migration files that would warrant upgrading this to a code change request. The suggestion remains classified as a suggestion -- no sub-task is created.' The report also confirms: '30002 | migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs:14 | suggestion | No sub-task (no convention match)'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'suggestion' and explicitly states 'The suggestion remains classified as a suggestion -- no sub-task is created.' report.md confirms: '30002 | migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs:14 | suggestion | No sub-task (no convention match)'. No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The assertion requires a sub-task regardless of path, but none was created.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row states: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 5 test functions have documentation comments. No repetitive test patterns detected. Eval Quality: N/A.' The 'Eval Quality: N/A' designation confirms eval quality was assessed as not applicable.
